page_title: "map_product function - functions"
subcategory: ""
description: |-
  Compute the Cartesian product of two string maps.
---

# function: map_product

Accepts two `map(string)` arguments and a separator string. Returns a `map(list(string))` whose keys are every combination of input keys joined by the separator, and whose values are two-element lists containing the corresponding values from each input map. For example, `map_product({a="v1"}, {b="v2"}, "|")` returns `{"a|b": ["v1", "v2"]}`.

## Example Usage

```terraform
output "map_product" {
  value = provider::functions::map_product(
    {
      privateDns1 = "storage"
      privateDns2 = "webapps"
    },
    {
      vNet1 = "office"
      vNet2 = "field"
    },
    ":"
  )
}

# map_product = tomap({
#   "privateDns1|vNet1" = tolist(["storage", "office"])
#   "privateDns1|vNet2" = tolist(["storage", "field"])
#   "privateDns2|vNet1" = tolist(["webapps", "office"])
#   "privateDns2|vNet2" = tolist(["webapps", "field"])
# })
```

## Signature

<!-- signature generated by tfplugindocs -->
```text
map_product(first map of string, second map of string, separator string) map of list of string
```

## Arguments

<!-- arguments generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `first` (Map of String) The first map(string). Its keys form the left-hand side of each product key.
1. `second` (Map of String) The second map(string). Its keys form the right-hand side of each product key.
1. `separator` (String) The string used to join a key from `first` and a key from `second`, e.g. `"|"` produces `"a|b"`.
